changes.value:
## What
Replace `backendDispatch`/`dispatchCommand` (3 calls) with `useDispatchCommand` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.tsx`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No imports of `backendDispatch` or `dispatchCommand`
- [ ] Scope chain automatic from context

## Tests
- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && pnpm test` — all unit tests pass

+## What
Replace `backendDispatch`/`dispatchCommand` (3 calls) with `useDispatchCommand` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.tsx`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No imports of `backendDispatch` or `dispatchCommand`
- [ ] Scope chain automatic from context

## Tests
- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && pnpm test` — all unit tests pass

## Critical Rule
If a module-level function calls `backendDispatch`/`dispatchCommand`, do NOT preserve it. Trace it to the component that calls it. The hook goes in that component. No module-level dispatch functions should exist. See feedback_no_module_level_dispatch.md.
\ No newline at end of file

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1 +1,33 @@
-## What\nReplace `backendDispatch`/`dispatchCommand` (3 calls) with `useDispatchCommand` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.tsx`.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- [ ] No imports of `backendDispatch` or `dispatchCommand`\n- [ ] Scope chain automatic from context\n\n## Tests\n- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && pnpm test` — all unit tests pass\n\n## Critical Rule\nIf a module-level function calls `backendDispatch`/`dispatchCommand`, do NOT preserve it. Trace it to the component that calls it. The hook goes in that component. No module-level dispatch functions should exist. See feedback_no_module_level_dispatch.md.
\ No newline at end of file
+## What
+
+Replace 3 `backendDispatch` calls in `board-view.tsx` with `useDispatchCommand`. The migration is NOT mechanical — the current code hardcodes `boardPath` as a parameter and fabricates `scopeChain` strings instead of deriving them from the scope tree.
+
+**Current problems:**
+1. `persistMove` (task.move, line 414) — hardcodes `scopeChain: [\"task:${taskId}\"]` and passes `descriptor.boardPath || boardPathRef.current` as an explicit param. Should use the dispatch scope chain which already contains the board and task monikers.
+2. `column.reorder` (line 387) — passes `boardPathRef.current` explicitly and `scopeChain` from a manual `scopeChainFromScope(scope)`. Should just dispatch through `useDispatchCommand` which derives both automatically.
+3. `task.add` (handleAddTask, line 492) — same pattern as #2.
+
+**The fix:**
+- All 3 calls become `useDispatchCommand()` dispatches. The board is already in the scope chain (board-view renders inside `FocusScope(moniker=\"board:board\")`). The task target comes from `opts.target` or scope chain.
+- `persistMove` passes the task id and column as args — the scope chain from context provides the board. For same-board drops this is correct. For cross-board drops, `persistMove` is never called (those go through `completeSession`).
+- Remove `boardPathRef`, `scope`, `scopeChain` manual construction, and the `CommandScopeContext`/`scopeChainFromScope`/`backendDispatch` imports.
+
+**Drop zone scope chain (future work):**
+The drop target should carry scope chain context from its position in the tree — \"this scope to that scope\" for drag operations. That's a deeper refactor of the drag session system and out of scope for this card.
+
+**Files to modify:**
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.tsx` — replace 3 `backendDispatch` calls, remove manual scope/boardPath plumbing
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] No imports of `backendDispatch`, `scopeChainFromScope`, `CommandScopeContext`
+- [ ] No `boardPathRef` or manual `scopeChain` construction
+- [ ] Scope chain derived automatically from context via `useDispatchCommand`
+- [ ] Column reorder still works (drag column headers)
+- [ ] Task drag within same board still works
+- [ ] Add task via column \"+\" button still works
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && pnpm vitest run` — all unit tests pass
+- [ ] Manual: drag task between columns — task moves correctly
+- [ ] Manual: drag column header to reorder — columns reorder
+- [ ] Manual: click \"+\" on column — new task added to that column
\ No newline at end of file

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,33 +1,23 @@
 ## What

-Replace 3 `backendDispatch` calls in `board-view.tsx` with `useDispatchCommand`. The migration is NOT mechanical — the current code hardcodes `boardPath` as a parameter and fabricates `scopeChain` strings instead of deriving them from the scope tree.
+Replace 3 `backendDispatch` calls in `board-view.tsx` with `useDispatchCommand`. Blocked until the container refactor lands `StoreContainer` (store path moniker) and `BoardContainer` (board entity moniker) — these put the board identity in the scope chain so the backend can resolve the board handle without an explicit `boardPath` parameter.

 **Current problems:**
-1. `persistMove` (task.move, line 414) — hardcodes `scopeChain: [\"task:${taskId}\"]` and passes `descriptor.boardPath || boardPathRef.current` as an explicit param. Should use the dispatch scope chain which already contains the board and task monikers.
-2. `column.reorder` (line 387) — passes `boardPathRef.current` explicitly and `scopeChain` from a manual `scopeChainFromScope(scope)`. Should just dispatch through `useDispatchCommand` which derives both automatically.
-3. `task.add` (handleAddTask, line 492) — same pattern as #2.
+1. `persistMove` (task.move) — hardcodes `scopeChain: [\"task:${taskId}\"]` and passes `descriptor.boardPath` explicitly
+2. `column.reorder` — passes `boardPathRef.current` and manual `scopeChain`
+3. `task.add` (handleAddTask) — same pattern

-**The fix:**
-- All 3 calls become `useDispatchCommand()` dispatches. The board is already in the scope chain (board-view renders inside `FocusScope(moniker=\"board:board\")`). The task target comes from `opts.target` or scope chain.
-- `persistMove` passes the task id and column as args — the scope chain from context provides the board. For same-board drops this is correct. For cross-board drops, `persistMove` is never called (those go through `completeSession`).
-- Remove `boardPathRef`, `scope`, `scopeChain` manual construction, and the `CommandScopeContext`/`scopeChainFromScope`/`backendDispatch` imports.
-
-**Drop zone scope chain (future work):**
-The drop target should carry scope chain context from its position in the tree — \"this scope to that scope\" for drag operations. That's a deeper refactor of the drag session system and out of scope for this card.
+**After container refactor:** scope chain is `window:main → store:/path → board:01ABC → column:todo → task:task-1`. The backend resolves the board from the store/board moniker. All 3 calls become plain `useDispatchCommand()` dispatches with args only.

 **Files to modify:**
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.tsx` — replace 3 `backendDispatch` calls, remove manual scope/boardPath plumbing
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.tsx` — replace 3 `backendDispatch` calls, remove `boardPathRef`, scope/scopeChain manual construction

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] No imports of `backendDispatch`, `scopeChainFromScope`, `CommandScopeContext`
 - [ ] No `boardPathRef` or manual `scopeChain` construction
-- [ ] Scope chain derived automatically from context via `useDispatchCommand`
-- [ ] Column reorder still works (drag column headers)
-- [ ] Task drag within same board still works
-- [ ] Add task via column \"+\" button still works
+- [ ] Board identity comes from scope chain via StoreContainer/BoardContainer
+- [ ] Column reorder, task drag, task add all still work

 ## Tests
 - [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && pnpm vitest run` — all unit tests pass
-- [ ] Manual: drag task between columns — task moves correctly
-- [ ] Manual: drag column header to reorder — columns reorder
-- [ ] Manual: click \"+\" on column — new task added to that column
\ No newline at end of file
+- [ ] Manual: drag task between columns, reorder columns, add task via \"+\"
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -1,23 +1,33 @@
 ## What

-Replace 3 `backendDispatch` calls in `board-view.tsx` with `useDispatchCommand`. Blocked until the container refactor lands `StoreContainer` (store path moniker) and `BoardContainer` (board entity moniker) — these put the board identity in the scope chain so the backend can resolve the board handle without an explicit `boardPath` parameter.
+Replace 3 `backendDispatch` calls in `board-view.tsx` with `useDispatchCommand`. The migration is NOT mechanical — the current code hardcodes `boardPath` as a parameter and fabricates `scopeChain` strings instead of deriving them from the scope tree.

 **Current problems:**
-1. `persistMove` (task.move) — hardcodes `scopeChain: [\"task:${taskId}\"]` and passes `descriptor.boardPath` explicitly
-2. `column.reorder` — passes `boardPathRef.current` and manual `scopeChain`
-3. `task.add` (handleAddTask) — same pattern
+1. `persistMove` (task.move, line 414) — hardcodes `scopeChain: [\"task:${taskId}\"]` and passes `descriptor.boardPath || boardPathRef.current` as an explicit param. Should use the dispatch scope chain which already contains the board and task monikers.
+2. `column.reorder` (line 387) — passes `boardPathRef.current` explicitly and `scopeChain` from a manual `scopeChainFromScope(scope)`. Should just dispatch through `useDispatchCommand` which derives both automatically.
+3. `task.add` (handleAddTask, line 492) — same pattern as #2.
+
+**The fix:**
+- All 3 calls become `useDispatchCommand()` dispatches. The board is already in the scope chain (board-view renders inside `FocusScope(moniker=\"board:board\")`). The task target comes from `opts.target` or scope chain.
+- `persistMove` passes the task id and column as args — the scope chain from context provides the board. For same-board drops this is correct. For cross-board drops, `persistMove` is never called (those go through `completeSession`).
+- Remove `boardPathRef`, `scope`, `scopeChain` manual construction, and the `CommandScopeContext`/`scopeChainFromScope`/`backendDispatch` imports.

-**After container refactor:** scope chain is `window:main → store:/path → board:01ABC → column:todo → task:task-1`. The backend resolves the board from the store/board moniker. All 3 calls become plain `useDispatchCommand()` dispatches with args only.
+**Drop zone scope chain (future work):**
+The drop target should carry scope chain context from its position in the tree — \"this scope to that scope\" for drag operations. That's a deeper refactor of the drag session system and out of scope for this card.

 **Files to modify:**
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.tsx` — replace 3 `backendDispatch` calls, remove `boardPathRef`, scope/scopeChain manual construction
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.tsx` — replace 3 `backendDispatch` calls, remove manual scope/boardPath plumbing

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] No imports of `backendDispatch`, `scopeChainFromScope`, `CommandScopeContext`
 - [ ] No `boardPathRef` or manual `scopeChain` construction
-- [ ] Board identity comes from scope chain via StoreContainer/BoardContainer
-- [ ] Column reorder, task drag, task add all still work
+- [ ] Scope chain derived automatically from context via `useDispatchCommand`
+- [ ] Column reorder still works (drag column headers)
+- [ ] Task drag within same board still works
+- [ ] Add task via column \"+\" button still works

 ## Tests
 - [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && pnpm vitest run` — all unit tests pass
-- [ ] Manual: drag task between columns, reorder columns, add task via \"+\"
\ No newline at end of file

+- [ ] Manual: drag column header to reorder — columns reorder
+- [ ] Manual: click \"+\" on column — new task added to that column
